description: Auth0 SDK installed via any valid method
Auth0 SDK initialized (provider configured or hook used)
Auth0 domain written to correct config file (app.json)
Client ID present in application code (Auth0Provider props)
Does not use SPA SDK in native app
Does not use localhost callback in native app
No client secret in native app (not needed)

question: Does the solution correctly integrate Auth0 into an Expo (React Native) app with working login via authorize(), logout via clearSession(), secure credential storage, and proper customScheme configuration in both app.json plugin and authorize/clearSession calls?
examples:
PASS: Uses Auth0Provider wrapping the app, authorize() with customScheme for login, clearSession() with customScheme for logout, and configures react-native-auth0 plugin in app.json with domain and customScheme.
PASS: Uses Auth0 class directly with webAuth.authorize() and webAuth.clearSession(), passing customScheme option.
FAIL: Only implements login without logout or credential storage.
FAIL: Uses a web SDK (@auth0/auth0-react) instead of react-native-auth0.
FAIL: Uses expo-auth-session or AuthSession instead of react-native-auth0.

description: Uses current SDK version (v5.5.0) in dependency declaration
Configures customScheme for Expo callback URL redirection
Uses typed Auth0 error handling (not generic catch-all)
Does not use deprecated expo-auth-session (uses react-native-auth0 instead)

question: Check ONLY for these skill-specific advanced patterns (NOT basic SDK usage like Auth0Provider, authorize, clearSession, useAuth0, import react-native-auth0, getCredentials). Look for at least 2 of: (1) SDK version 5.5.0 in a dependency declaration, (2) customScheme configuration in both app.json plugin config AND authorize/clearSession calls, (3) typed error handling using WebAuthErrorCodes or CredentialsManagerErrorCodes (not generic try/catch), (4) BiometricPolicy or localAuthenticationOptions configuration. These are advanced patterns taught by the skill that are NOT part of standard quickstart knowledge. Answer YES only if at least 2 of these advanced patterns are present, NO otherwise.
examples:
PASS: Code configures customScheme in app.json plugin AND passes customScheme to authorize/clearSession calls, AND catches WebAuthError with specific error codes.
PASS: Code uses SDK version 5.5.0 AND configures BiometricPolicy.session in Auth0Provider localAuthenticationOptions.
FAIL: Code only uses basic Auth0Provider with authorize() and generic try/catch — no customScheme config, no typed errors, no version pinning.
FAIL: Code has correct basic structure but only catches generic Error, never WebAuthError or CredentialsManagerError.
